Family.title: Family
Family.description: Represents a family unit in the app, allowing multiple members to be linked and share resources.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Family entity.

familyName.description: The name of the family unit, e.g., 'Sharma Parivar'.

headOfFamilyUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who is designated as the head of the family. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Family)

memberUserIds.description: References to UserProfiles belonging to this family. (Relationship: Family N:N UserProfile)

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the family unit was registered.

languagePreferences.description: A list of preferred languages for family-wide content and interactions.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents an individual user's profile, containing personal details, preferences, and links to family if applicable.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

familyId.description: Reference to the Family this user belongs to, if any. (Relationship: Family 1:N UserProfile)

roleInFamily.description: The user's role within their family (e.g., 'पिता', 'माता', 'पुत्र', 'पुत्री').

spiritualInterestIds.description: References to Religions representing the user's spiritual interests. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Religion)

generalInterests.description: A list of general interests of the user (e.g., 'Physics', 'Astronomy', 'Cricket').

educationLevel.description: The user's current education level or highest attained (e.g., '11th Grade', 'Graduate').

religiousAffiliationId.description: Reference to the primary Religion the user identifies with. (Relationship: Religion 1:N UserProfile)

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile image.

Conversation.title: Conversation
Conversation.description: Stores a thread of communication between a user (or family) and the AI or a human Guru.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Conversation entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated or is participating in this conversation. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Conversation)

startedAt.description: The date and time when the conversation was initiated.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The date and time when the last message was added to the conversation.

type.description: The type of conversation (e.g., 'AI Guidance', 'Crisis Counseling', 'General Query').

status.description: The current status of the conversation (e.g., 'Open', 'Closed', 'Pending Guru Review').

Message.title: Message
Message.description: An individual message within a conversation thread.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

conversationId.description: Reference to the Conversation this message belongs to. (Relationship: Conversation 1:N Message)

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile or Guru who sent the message. This can be null if the sender is 'AI'. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Message, Guru 1:N Message)

content.description: The actual text content of the message.

timestamp.description: The date and time when the message was sent.

ChatChannel.title: ChatChannel
ChatChannel.description: Represents a direct message channel between two or more users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the channel.

participantIds.description: An array of UserProfile IDs participating in the channel.

lastMessage.description: A snippet of the last message sent.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: An individual message within a chat channel.

KnowledgeArticle.title: KnowledgeArticle
KnowledgeArticle.description: Contains informational content related to spirituality, ethics, science, and cultural practices, suitable for various audiences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the KnowledgeArticle entity.

title.description: The title of the knowledge article.

summary.description: A brief summary of the article's content.

fullContent.description: The complete content of the article, possibly in rich text or markdown format.

religionIds.description: References to Religions relevant to this article. (Relationship: KnowledgeArticle N:N Religion)

topicTags.description: A list of relevant keywords or concepts (e.g., 'sin', 'karma', 'Big Bang').

category.description: The category of the article (e.g., 'Science and Religion', 'Teen Psychology', 'Career Guidance').

targetAudiences.description: A list of intended audience groups (e.g., 'Children', 'Teenagers', 'Parents', 'Gurus').

mediaUrls.description: URLs to associated images, videos, or audio files.

publicationDate.description: The date and time when the article was published.

author.description: The author or source of the article (e.g., 'Swami Ramdev').

Religion.title: Religion
Religion.description: Represents a distinct religious or philosophical tradition supported by the app, providing metadata and symbols.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Religion entity.

description.description: A brief description of the religion.

symbolUrl.description: URL to an icon or symbol representing the religion (e.g., 'Om', 'Crescent', 'Cross').

keyBeliefs.description: A list of core beliefs or tenets of the religion.

keyTexts.description: A list of important scriptures or texts associated with the religion (e.g., 'Bhagavad Gita', 'Quran').

DailyWisdom.title: DailyWisdom
DailyWisdom.description: Daily thoughts, spiritual affirmations, or inspiring quotes provided by the AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DailyWisdom entity.

quote.description: The text of the daily wisdom quote or thought.

source.description: The source of the quote (e.g., 'Srimad Bhagavad Gita', 'Unknown').

theme.description: The primary theme of the wisdom (e.g., 'Karma', 'Peace', 'Love').

religionIds.description: References to Religions that align with this wisdom. (Relationship: DailyWisdom N:N Religion)

targetAudience.description: The intended audience for this wisdom (e.g., 'All', 'Children', 'Gurus').

datePublished.description: The date for which this daily wisdom is scheduled to be displayed.

Story.title: Story
Story.description: Educational or moral stories, particularly curated for children of different age groups.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Story entity.

content.description: The full narrative content of the story, possibly in rich text or markdown.

ageGroup.description: The target age group for the story (e.g., '3-5 years', '6-8 years', '9-12 years').

moralLesson.description: The underlying moral or ethical lesson conveyed by the story.

religionIds.description: References to Religions whose teachings align with the story. (Relationship: Story N:N Religion)

mediaUrls.description: URLs to associated audio, video, or image files for the story.

Activity.title: Activity
Activity.description: Structured activities for individuals or families, including games, exercises, and guided practices related to spiritual and ethical development.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Activity entity.

name.description: The name of the activity (e.g., 'Family Meditation', 'Ethics Puzzle', 'Coloring Page').

description.description: A detailed description of the activity and its purpose.

type.description: The category or type of activity (e.g., 'Meditation', 'Yoga', 'Puzzle', 'Coloring', 'Chanting').

targetAgeGroup.description: The target age group for the activity (e.g., 'All', 'Children', 'Adults').

estimatedDurationMinutes.description: The estimated time required to complete the activity, in minutes.

materialsNeeded.description: A list of materials or resources required for the activity.

religionIds.description: References to Religions that this activity is relevant to. (Relationship: Activity N:N Religion)

instructionUrl.description: URL to detailed instructions, a guide, or a video for the activity.

Goal.title: Goal
Goal.description: Tracks personal or family goals related to spiritual practice, well-being, or learning within the app.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Goal entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile for whom this goal is set. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Goal)

familyId.description: Reference to the Family for whom this goal is set, if it's a family-wide goal. (Relationship: Family 1:N Goal)

description.description: A detailed description of the goal (e.g., 'Meditate 10 minutes daily').

targetValue.description: The target quantity or benchmark for the goal (e.g., '10 minutes', 'one article').

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the target value (e.g., 'minutes', 'article', 'times').

frequency.description: How often the goal should be pursued (e.g., 'daily', 'weekly', 'monthly').

startDate.description: The date when the goal started.

endDate.description: The optional end date for the goal.

status.description: The current status of the goal (e.g., 'Active', 'Completed', 'Paused').

progressRecords.description: A list of progress records, each containing a date and the achieved value for that period.

ForumTopic.title: ForumTopic
ForumTopic.description: Represents a discussion topic or thread in the community forum.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ForumTopic entity.

title.description: The title of the forum topic.

description.description: A brief description or the initial question of the topic.

creatorUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this topic. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ForumTopic)

createdAt.description: The date and time when the topic was created.

lastPostAt.description: The date and time of the most recent post in this topic.

tags.description: A list of tags or keywords associated with the topic.

totalPosts.description: The total number of posts in this topic (excluding comments).

totalComments.description: The total number of comments across all posts in this topic.

ForumPost.title: ForumPost
ForumPost.description: A primary post within a community forum topic, which can receive comments and reactions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ForumPost entity.

forumTopicId.description: Reference to the ForumTopic this post belongs to. (Relationship: ForumTopic 1:N ForumPost)

authorUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who authored this post. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ForumPost)

content.description: The full content of the forum post, possibly in rich text or markdown.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the post was created.

likes.description: The number of likes received by this post.

likedBy.description: Array of user IDs who have liked this post.

emotionalReactions.description: An object detailing various emotional reactions and their counts for the post.

isBestPostOfTheWeek.description: Indicates if this post has been highlighted as the best post of the week.

ForumComment.title: ForumComment
ForumComment.description: A comment made on a specific forum post.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ForumComment entity.

forumPostId.description: Reference to the ForumPost this comment is attached to. (Relationship: ForumPost 1:N ForumComment)

authorUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who authored this comment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ForumComment)

content.description: The text content of the comment.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the comment was created.

likes.description: The number of likes received by this comment.

CommunityEvent.title: CommunityEvent
CommunityEvent.description: Details about upcoming community gatherings, workshops, or online sessions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CommunityEvent entity.

name.description: The name of the event (e.g., 'Online Satsang', 'Parents Counseling Camp').

description.description: A detailed description of the event.

startDate.description: The start date and time of the event.

endDate.description: The end date and time of the event.

location.description: The physical or virtual location of the event (e.g., 'Online', 'Delhi', 'Mumbai').

organizerUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile or Guru organizing the event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N CommunityEvent, Guru 1:N CommunityEvent)

isFree.description: Indicates whether the event is free of charge.

Guru.title: Guru
Guru.description: Represents a certified human 'Kulaguru' who provides specialized guidance and potentially manages multiple families.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Guru entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the individual who is a Guru. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Guru)

specializations.description: A list of areas of expertise or specialization (e.g., 'Family Counseling', 'Crisis Management').

languageSkills.description: A list of languages the Guru is proficient in for counseling.

certifiedCourseModuleIds.description: References to CourseModules for which the Guru is certified. (Relationship: Guru N:N CourseModule)

overallRating.description: The average rating received by the Guru from users, on a scale.

familiesManagedCount.description: The number of families currently being managed or counseled by this Guru.

city.description: The city where the Guru is located, for geographical search.

isApproved.description: Indicates if the Guru's profile and certification are approved by the platform.

TrainingCourse.title: TrainingCourse
TrainingCourse.description: Defines a structured curriculum for Guru training and certification.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TrainingCourse entity.

name.description: The name of the training course (e.g., 'Guru Certification Program').

description.description: A detailed description of the course content and objectives.

durationYears.description: The total duration of the course in years.

targetAudience.description: The intended audience for the course (e.g., 'Aspiring Gurus', 'Experienced Counselors').

courseModuleIds.description: References to CourseModules that constitute this training course. (Relationship: TrainingCourse N:N CourseModule)

CourseModule.title: CourseModule
CourseModule.description: A distinct educational module or unit within a larger Guru training course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CourseModule entity.

name.description: The name of the module (e.g., 'Philosophy of Religions', 'Psychology and Counseling').

description.description: A detailed description of the module's learning objectives and content.

syllabusTopics.description: A list of key topics covered within the module.

resourceArticleIds.description: References to KnowledgeArticles or other learning resources for this module. (Relationship: CourseModule N:N KnowledgeArticle)

Festival.title: Festival
Festival.description: Information about religious festivals across different faiths, including their significance and timing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Festival entity.

description.description: A brief explanation of the festival's history and significance.

date.description: The primary date of the festival (may vary by year or calculation).

religionIds.description: References to Religions that observe this festival. (Relationship: Festival N:N Religion)

suggestedActivityIds.description: References to Activities that are suggested for this festival. (Relationship: Festival N:N Activity)

Testimonial.title: Testimonial
Testimonial.description: User feedback or success stories displayed on the landing page or other promotional sections.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Testimonial entity.

quote.description: The actual testimonial quote from the user.

authorName.description: The name of the author of the testimonial (e.g., 'Priya, Delhi').

authorUserId.description: Optional reference to the UserProfile who provided the testimonial. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Testimonial)

authorLocation.description: The location of the testimonial author (e.g., 'Delhi').

dateSubmitted.description: The date and time when the testimonial was submitted.

status.description: The moderation status of the testimonial (e.g., 'Approved', 'Pending', 'Rejected').

CrisisCounselingTopic.title: CrisisCounselingTopic
CrisisCounselingTopic.description: Categories of common crises or problems for which users might seek immediate counseling or guidance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CrisisCounselingTopic entity.

name.description: The name of the crisis topic (e.g., 'Family Conflict', 'Financial Stress', 'Grief').

description.description: A brief description of what this crisis topic entails.

relatedArticleIds.description: References to KnowledgeArticles that provide deeper insights or background on this crisis. (Relationship: CrisisCounselingTopic N:N KnowledgeArticle)

suggestedImmediateActions.description: A list of immediate, actionable advice or steps for this crisis.

CrisisGuidance.title: CrisisGuidance
CrisisGuidance.description: Specific advice and resources tailored to a crisis topic, potentially filtered by religious perspective.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CrisisGuidance entity.

crisisTopicId.description: Reference to the CrisisCounselingTopic this guidance is for. (Relationship: CrisisCounselingTopic 1:N CrisisGuidance)

religionId.description: Optional reference to a Religion for religious-specific guidance. (Relationship: Religion 1:N CrisisGuidance)

guidanceText.description: The general AI-generated guidance or message for the crisis.

religiousPerspectiveText.description: Text providing a specific religious perspective on the crisis, if applicable.

quickReliefActivityIds.description: References to Activities offering quick relief or comfort (e.g., meditation, breathing exercises). (Relationship: CrisisGuidance N:N Activity)

humanSupportLink.description: URL or contact information for seeking human professional help.

GuruTrainingProgress.title: GuruTrainingProgress
GuruTrainingProgress.description: Tracks a specific Guru's progress through individual training modules of a course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GuruTrainingProgress entity.

guruId.description: Reference to the Guru whose progress is being tracked. (Relationship: Guru 1:N GuruTrainingProgress)

courseModuleId.description: Reference to the CourseModule being progressed through. (Relationship: CourseModule 1:N GuruTrainingProgress)

status.description: The current status of the Guru's progress in this module (e.g., 'Not Started', 'In Progress', 'Completed').

completionDate.description: The date when the module was completed, if applicable.

progressPercentage.description: The percentage of completion for the module (0-100).

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID corresponds to the user's Firebase Authentication UID. Access is restricted to the owner. The 'id' field within the document matches the {userId} wildcard.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores family units. The document ID is a unique family identifier. Access is restricted to members of the family, as defined by the 'memberUserIds' array. The 'id' field within the document matches the {familyId} wildcard.

params.name: familyId
params.description: The unique ID of the family unit.

definition.description: Stores individual user conversations with AI or Gurus. Nested under the owning user's profile. Access is restricted to the owning user. The 'id' field within the document matches the {conversationId} wildcard. The 'userId' field within the document is redundant with the path but is maintained for schema consistency and simplified rule writing (e.g., `resource.data.userId == userId`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this conversation.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique ID of the conversation.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within a conversation. Nested under its parent conversation. Includes denormalized 'conversationOwnerId' (from the parent user) for authorization independence, allowing direct rule evaluation without 'get()'. The 'id' field within the document matches the {messageId} wildcard. The 'conversationId' field within the document is redundant with the path but is maintained for schema consistency and simplified rule writing (e.g., `resource.data.conversationId == conversationId`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent conversation.

params.name: conversationId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent conversation.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the message.

definition.description: Stores direct message channels. Access is restricted to participants listed in the 'participantIds' array.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique ID of the chat channel.

definition.description: Stores individual messages within a chat channel. Access is restricted to participants of the parent channel.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent channel.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the message.

definition.description: Stores informational content. Publicly readable. Admin writable. The 'id' field within the document matches the {articleId} wildcard.

params.name: articleId
params.description: The unique ID of the knowledge article.

definition.description: Stores definitions and metadata for different religions. Publicly readable. Admin writable. The 'id' field within the document matches the {religionId} wildcard.

params.name: religionId
params.description: The unique ID of the religion.

definition.description: Stores daily wisdom quotes and affirmations. Publicly readable. Admin writable. The 'id' field within the document matches the {wisdomId} wildcard.

params.name: wisdomId
params.description: The unique ID of the daily wisdom entry.

definition.description: Stores educational and moral stories, primarily for children. Publicly readable. Admin writable. The 'id' field within the document matches the {storyId} wildcard.

params.name: storyId
params.description: The unique ID of the story.

definition.description: Stores structured activities for individuals or families. Publicly readable. Admin writable. The 'id' field within the document matches the {activityId} wildcard.

params.name: activityId
params.description: The unique ID of the activity.

definition.description: Stores personal goals for individual users. Nested under the user's profile. Access restricted to the owning user. The 'id' field within the document matches the {goalId} wildcard. The 'userId' field in the document (if present) is redundant with the path, but ensures path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this goal.

params.name: goalId
params.description: The unique ID of the personal goal.

definition.description: Stores family-wide goals. Nested under the family unit. Includes denormalized 'authorizedFamilyMembers' (an array of UIDs from the parent family) for authorization independence, allowing all family members to access without 'get()'. The 'id' field within the document matches the {goalId} wildcard. The 'familyId' field in the document (if present) is redundant with the path, but simplifies authorization rules based on family membership.

params.name: familyId
params.description: The unique ID of the family unit that owns this goal.

params.name: goalId
params.description: The unique ID of the family goal.

definition.description: Stores discussion topics for the community forum. Publicly readable. 'creatorUserId' dictates write/delete access for the creator. The 'id' field within the document matches the {topicId} wildcard.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique ID of the forum topic.

definition.description: Stores individual posts within a forum topic. Nested under the parent forum topic. Publicly readable. 'authorUserId' dictates write/delete access for the author. The 'id' field within the document matches the {postId} wildcard. The 'forumTopicId' field within the document is redundant with the path but is maintained for schema consistency and simplified rule writing.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent forum topic.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique ID of the forum post.

definition.description: Stores comments on forum posts. Nested under the parent forum post. Publicly readable. 'authorUserId' dictates write/delete access for the author. The 'id' field within the document matches the {commentId} wildcard. The 'forumPostId' field within the document is redundant with the path but is maintained for schema consistency and simplified rule writing.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent forum topic.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent forum post.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique ID of the comment.

definition.description: Stores details about upcoming community events. Publicly readable. 'organizerUserId' dictates write/delete access for the organizer. The 'id' field within the document matches the {eventId} wildcard.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique ID of the community event.

definition.description: Stores profiles of certified human Gurus. Publicly readable. Gurus can update their own profile (where guruId matches their userId), and platform administrators can manage all Guru profiles (especially 'isApproved' status). The 'id' field within the document matches the {guruId} wildcard.

params.name: guruId
params.description: The unique ID of the Guru, typically matching their UserProfile ID.

definition.description: Stores definitions of Guru training courses. Publicly readable. Admin writable. The 'id' field within the document matches the {courseId} wildcard.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the training course.

definition.description: Stores individual modules within a training course. Nested under the parent training course. Publicly readable. Admin writable. The 'id' field within the document matches the {moduleId} wildcard. The 'trainingCourseId' field within the document is redundant with the path but is maintained for schema consistency and simplified rule writing.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent training course.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique ID of the course module.

definition.description: Stores information about religious festivals. Publicly readable. Admin writable. The 'id' field within the document matches the {festivalId} wildcard.

params.name: festivalId
params.description: The unique ID of the festival.

definition.description: Stores testimonials that have been approved for public display. Publicly readable. Only administrators can write (setting status to 'Approved'). The 'id' field within the document matches the {testimonialId} wildcard.

params.name: testimonialId
params.description: The unique ID of the approved testimonial.

definition.description: Stores testimonials awaiting moderation (status 'Pending'). Accessible only by administrators for review and moderation. The 'id' field within the document matches the {testimonialId} wildcard. A user can read/write their own pending testimonial if 'authorUserId' matches their UID.

params.name: testimonialId
params.description: The unique ID of the pending testimonial.

definition.description: Stores categories of crisis topics for counseling. Publicly readable. Admin writable. The 'id' field within the document matches the {topicId} wildcard.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique ID of the crisis counseling topic.

definition.description: Stores specific guidance and resources for crisis topics. Nested under the parent crisis topic. Publicly readable. Admin writable. The 'id' field within the document matches the {guidanceId} wildcard. The 'crisisTopicId' field within the document is redundant with the path but is maintained for schema consistency and simplified rule writing.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent crisis counseling topic.

params.name: guidanceId
params.description: The unique ID of the crisis guidance.

definition.description: Tracks a specific Guru's progress through individual training modules. Nested under the Guru's profile. Accessible by the owning Guru and administrators. Includes 'guruId' and 'courseModuleId' as explicit fields, with 'guruId' matching the path parameter for clear ownership. The 'id' field within the document matches the {progressId} wildcard.

params.name: guruId
params.description: The unique ID of the Guru whose training progress is being tracked.

params.name: progressId
params.description: The unique ID for this specific training progress record.

definition.description: A dedicated collection to identify platform administrators. The document ID is the UID of an administrator. The existence of a document signifies admin privileges, enabling authorization independence without custom claims. Not directly linked to a UserProfile entity in the sense of storing full profile data, but refers to a UserProfile's UID.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of a user with administrator privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization):**
*   **User Profiles (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored as top-level documents, with the document ID matching the `request.auth.uid`. This provides direct, path-based ownership, making authorization rules for a user's own data extremely straightforward (`allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`).
*   **Family Data (`/families/{familyId}`):** Families are also top-level entities. The `memberUserIds` array within the `Family` document is critical for authorization. For any subcollection or related data (like family goals) whose access depends on family membership, this `memberUserIds` array MUST be denormalized into the child document itself (e.g., as `authorizedFamilyMembers` or `familyMembers`). This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent documents, thus ensuring atomic operations and simplifying rule logic. For instance, a rule for a family goal document would directly check `request.auth.uid in resource.data.familyMembers`.
*   **Conversations and Messages:** Personal conversations are strictly nested under the user (`/users/{userId}/conversations/{conversationId}`). For messages within these conversations, the `conversationOwnerId` (the `userId` from the parent conversation) is denormalized into each `Message` document. This allows a message's security rule to directly verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.conversationOwnerId` without needing to `get()` the parent conversation.
*   **Goals:** Goals are segregated into two distinct paths: `/users/{userId}/goals/{goalId}` for personal goals and `/families/{familyId}/goals/{goalId}` for family goals. For family goals, the `memberUserIds` from the associated `Family` document must be denormalized into the `Goal` document (e.g., as `authorizedFamilyMembers`) to grant access to all family members without a `get()` call. Personal goals naturally use path-based ownership.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
*   **Testimonials:** To manage different access requirements (public vs. admin review), testimonials are segregated. `public_testimonials` contains only 'Approved' testimonials accessible by everyone. `pending_testimonials` is a separate collection strictly for 'Pending' testimonials, accessible only by administrators for review and moderation. This prevents mixing public and sensitive (unreviewed) data in the same collection, simplifying rules and client-side filtering.
*   **Public Content:** Entities like `KnowledgeArticle`, `Religion`, `DailyWisdom`, `Story`, `Activity`, `TrainingCourse`, `CourseModule`, `Festival`, `CrisisCounselingTopic`, and `CrisisGuidance` are placed in top-level collections (or nested under other public collections) with public read access. This structural clarity immediately indicates their open accessibility.
*   **Admin Roles:** A dedicated `admin_roles/{uid}` collection is used to store administrator UIDs. The existence of a document for a given `uid` in this collection grants admin privileges. This cleanly separates administrative roles from other user data and allows rules to check `exists(/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid))` for admin authorization.

**Queryable Authorization Patterns (QAPs):**
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** For `UserProfile`, `Conversation`, `Message`, and `UserGoal` documents, the `userId` in the path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/...`) directly aligns with `request.auth.uid`, enabling efficient and secure queries for a user's private data. This intrinsically supports QAPs, as list operations automatically constrain results to the authenticated user's scope.
*   **Membership Maps/Arrays:** For `Family` and `FamilyGoal` documents, denormalized `memberUserIds` (or `authorizedFamilyMembers`) allow `list` queries with `.where('familyMembers', 'array-contains', request.auth.uid)`. This effectively filters documents to only those the user is authorized for, thus supporting QAPs for collaborative data.
*   **Public Collections:** All public collections (e.g., `/knowledgeArticles`, `/religions`) are designed for open read access, which naturally supports unrestricted list operations for general content.
*   **Admin Filtering:** The `admin_roles` collection, combined with specific security rules, ensures that only authorized administrators can list and manage privileged collections (`pending_testimonials`, certain aspects of `Guru` profiles, and write access to public content collections).

By consistently applying these principles, the Firestore structure ensures that security rules are minimal, explicit, and easily auditable, reducing the likelihood of vulnerabilities and simplifying debugging. Each document carries its authorization context, making rules direct and avoiding complex cross-document lookups.
